allegation: Malicious communications, contrary to section 1 of the Malicious Communications Act 1988

courtLine: The defence asks the court to record that platform disclosure, handle mapping, and IP/subscriber attribution remain outstanding before account attribution is treated as fixed.
clientSummaryPreview:
CLIENT-SAFE SUMMARY
(not for court or CPS)

We are reviewing the papers in your case (Nadia Pike). This is early-stage — nothing is final until we have full disclosure and your instructions. Social/handle material may be on the papers, but platform disclosure, handle-to-defendant mapping, and IP/subscriber data remain outstanding. A handle alone does not prove account attribution.

[CaseBrain — client-safe summary. Evidence state: provisional. Not for court or CPS use.]
doNotOverstate: Do not treat a social handle alone as proof of subscriber or account attribution.
